fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Strong revenue growth and healthy cash flow are offset by unprofitability and high valuation multiples.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 11.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY — robust top-line expansion.
Free cash flow $463.32M TTM — generating cash from operations.
Consensus buy rating from 36 analysts with a mean target of $362.24.
negatives: Profit margin -2.9% and Operating margin 0.0% — company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity -2.5% and Return on assets -2.4% — negative returns on capital.
Forward P/E 43.10x — valuation is demanding given current profitability.

news.paragraph: MongoDB reported Q1 results that surpassed both earnings and revenue estimates, with year-over-year growth noted. Despite beating expectations and seeing positive analyst commentary including a raised price target from Barclays, the stock saw a pause in its upward momentum. The company's AI gains were mentioned, but the market reaction suggests a mixed sentiment as traders digest the news.

headlines.title: MongoDB Q1 Earnings & Revenues Surpass Estimates, Increases Y/Y
MongoDB Beats Earnings, But Shares Hit Pause Despite AI Gains - MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB)
